Package de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.label

Class OriginTermLabel

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.label.OriginTermLabel
All Implemented Interfaces:
TermLabel, Named
public class OriginTermLabel extends Object implements TermLabel

An OriginTermLabel saves a term's origin in the JML specification (getOrigin()) as well as the origins of all of its subterms and former subterms (getSubtermOrigins()).

For this to work correctly, you must call #collectSubtermOrigins(Term, TermBuilder) for every top-level formula in your original proof obligation.

Before doing this, you can call TermBuilder.addLabelToAllSubs(Term, TermLabel) for every term you have added to the original contract in your PO to add an OriginTermLabel of your choosing. Terms for which you do not do this get a label of the form new OriginTermLabel(SpecType.NONE, null, -1).

    Origin for terms that originate from a file.

    static class 
    OriginTermLabel.Origin
    An origin encapsulates some information about where a term originates from.
    static enum 
    OriginTermLabel.SpecType
    A SpecType is any type of JML specification which gets translated into JavaDL.

    • OriginTermLabel

      public OriginTermLabel(OriginTermLabel.Origin origin)
      Creates a new OriginTermLabel.
      Parameters:
      origin - the term's origin.

    • OriginTermLabel

      public OriginTermLabel(OriginTermLabel.Origin origin, Set<OriginTermLabel.Origin> subtermOrigins)
      Creates a new OriginTermLabel.
      Parameters:
      origin - the term's origin.
      subtermOrigins - the origins of the term's (former) subterms.

    • OriginTermLabel

      public OriginTermLabel(Set<OriginTermLabel.Origin> subtermOrigins)
      Creates a new OriginTermLabel.
      Parameters:
      subtermOrigins - the origins of the term's (former) subterms.

  • Method Details

    • getOrigin

      public static OriginTermLabel.Origin getOrigin(PosInSequent pis)
      Find a term's origin. If the term has no origin, iterate through its parent terms until we find one with an origin.
      Parameters:
      pis - the position of the term whose origin to find.
      Returns:
      the term's origin, or the origin of one of its parents.

    • getOrigin

      public static OriginTermLabel.Origin getOrigin(PosInOccurrence pio)
      Find a term's origin. If the term has no origin, iterate through its parent terms until we find one with an origin.
      Parameters:
      pio - the position of the term whose origin to find.
      Returns:
      the term's origin, or the origin of one of its parents.

    • equals

      Overrides:
      equals in class Object

    • canAddLabel

      public static boolean canAddLabel(Term term, Services services)

      Determines whether an OriginTermLabel can be added to the specified term.

      E.g., no labels should be added to terms whose operator is a heap variable as this leads to various problems during proof search.

      Parameters:
      term - a term
      services - services.
      Returns:
      true iff an OriginTermLabel can be added to the specified term.

    • canAddLabel

      public static boolean canAddLabel(Operator op, Services services)

      Determines whether an OriginTermLabel can be added to a term with the specified operator.

      E.g., no labels should be added to terms whose operator is a heap variable as this leads to various problems during proof search.

      Parameters:
      op - the specified operator.
      services - services.
      Returns:
      true iff an OriginTermLabel can be added to a term with the specified operator.

    • removeOriginLabels

      public static SequentChangeInfo removeOriginLabels(Sequent seq, Services services)
      Removes all OriginTermLabel from the specified sequent.
      Parameters:
      seq - the sequent to transform.
      services - services.
      Returns:
      the resulting sequent change info.

    • removeOriginLabels

      public static Term removeOriginLabels(Term term, Services services)
      Removes all OriginTermLabel from the specified term and its sub-terms.
      Parameters:
      term - the term to transform.
      services - services.
      Returns:
      the transformed term.

      Returns:

    • collectSubtermOrigins

      public static Term collectSubtermOrigins(Term term, Services services)
      This method transforms a term in such a way that every OriginTermLabel contains all of the correct getSubtermOrigins().
      Parameters:
      term - the term to transform.
      services - services.
      Returns:
      the transformed term.

    • getChild

      public Object getChild(int i)
      Description copied from interface: TermLabel
      Retrieves the i-th parameter object of this term label.

      A term label may have structure, i.e. can be parameterized.

      Specified by:
      getChild in interface TermLabel
      Parameters:
      i - the number of the parameter to retrieve ( 0 <= i < getChildCount())
      Returns:
      the selected parameter

    • getChildCount

      public int getChildCount()
      Description copied from interface: TermLabel
      Gets the number of parameters of this term label.
      Specified by:
      getChildCount in interface TermLabel
      Returns:
      the number of parameters (a non-negative number)

    • isProofRelevant

      public boolean isProofRelevant()
      Description copied from interface: TermLabel
      Returns true iff this label is used in any way during the proof. E.g., OriginTermLabels are not used during the proof; they only provide a convenience for the user.
      Specified by:
      isProofRelevant in interface TermLabel
      Returns:
      true iff this label is used in any way during the proof.

    • getOrigin

      public OriginTermLabel.Origin getOrigin()
      Returns:
      the term's origin.

    • getSubtermOrigins

      public Set<OriginTermLabel.Origin> getSubtermOrigins()

      Returns the origins of the term's sub-terms and former sub-terms.

      Note that you need to have called #collectSubtermOrigins(Term, TermBuilder) for this method to work correctly.

      Returns:
      the origins of the term's sub-terms and former sub-terms.
      See Also:
      • OriginTermLabelRefactoring
